text:
Once upon a time in the charming town of Blossom Cove, there lived a wise old owl named Olive. She spun incredible webs at nights for robes and bedding. One particular evening, while she spun yarn as usual, she heard delightful giggling.

Looking up, she saw a group of butterflies. They seemed excited about something. One buzzed wildly.

'Owl owl owl. Tell a story.' It fluttered closer.

'Oops! Maybe I shouldn.'

'Ollie. You scare me.'

'O-okay. Once upon a time...' She recounted adventures of brave squirrels and silly turtles.

The butterflies loved it! They danced around her. After storytime, one asked.

'There’s one more.'

'Ooh yeah?'

'Oooo! Why did the timid snail win the sprint?'

The snail just kept going! she laughed.

'Eee! Like that!' They spun around.

Olive clucked amusedly.

'I think you're speedy butterflies.'
